plannedNodes.name: Atlas Engine Root
plannedNodes.purpose: Primary entry point and Composition Root for the backend engine.
plannedNodes.description: Atlas Architect Root: The primary entry point and Composition Root for the backend engine. DESIGN INTENT: This module orchestrates the entire lifecycle of the Atlas service. It is responsible for: 1. System Bootstrapping: Initializing configuration, session management, and the shared registry. 2. Composition Root: Assembling the core engine (FileScanner, GraphBuilder, LayoutStrategy). 3. API Gateway: Serving the REST API for topology data, node management, and visualizer assets. 4. Codebase Watcher: Monitoring the target project for source changes to trigger automated rescans. 5. Broadcast Hub: Managing real-time Server-Sent Events (SSE) to keep connected clients synchronized. All topological data flows through this root before being persisted to .atlas data.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Orchestrates the service lifecycle, including bootstrapping, core engine assembly (Scanner, Graph, Layout), API Gateway, and real-time broadcasting via SSE.

plannedNodes.name: Heartbeat Monitor
plannedNodes.purpose: System health monitoring and periodic pulse broadcasting.
plannedNodes.description: HeartbeatService: System health monitoring and pulse broadcasting. DESIGN INTENT: Provides a minimal, low-overhead signal to confirm the Atlas Engine process is alive and its internal timer loop is functioning. It serves as the "Vital Signs" monitor for the backend, ensuring that background tasks (like the Watcher) remain active. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Emits a periodic, timestamped "System Nominal" signal to the console. 2. Acts as a baseline verification point for system uptime. 3. Provides a hook for future health-monitoring integrations (e.g., external watchdog).
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provides a minimal, low-overhead signal to confirm the Atlas Engine process is alive and its internal timer loop is functioning. Serves as the 'Vital Signs' monitor.

plannedNodes.name: Atlas Engine
plannedNodes.purpose: Core orchestration unit responsible for the Scan - Build - Layout pipeline.
plannedNodes.description: AtlasEngine: The core orchestration unit of the system. DESIGN INTENT: Acts as the "Brain" of the Atlas ecosystem. It bridges the gap between raw file system data and high-level architectural models. It implements the drift detection logic by comparing the scanned 'Reality' against the historical 'Registry'. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Orchestrates the Scan -> Build -> Layout -> Validate pipeline. 2. Manages Drift Detection: Identifies 'Dirty' nodes where implementation violates verified state. 3. Contract Ripple: Detects changes in public interfaces and invalidates dependents. 4. Architectural Linting: Enforces "Iron Law" rules defined in the system config.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Acts as the 'Brain' of the Atlas ecosystem. It bridges the gap between raw file system data and high-level architectural models. It implements the drift detection logic by comparing the scanned 'Reality' against the historical 'Registry'.

plannedNodes.name: FileScanner
plannedNodes.purpose: Deep-tissue source code extractor and crawler.
plannedNodes.description: FileScanner: Deep-tissue source code extractor. DESIGN INTENT: Provides the "Eyes" of the Atlas engine. It is responsible for crawling the physical filesystem and extracting structured metadata from source files. It abstracts away the complexities of globbing, file exclusion, and multi-language parsing. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Crawls the project directory using configurable inclusion exclusion patterns. 2. Delegates parsing to language-specific parsers via the ParserFactory. 3. Merges results to ensure consistent node identification across the project. 4. Normalizes file paths and manages the initial extraction of dependencies and metrics.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provides the 'Eyes' of the Atlas engine. It crawls the physical filesystem and extracts structured metadata from source files, abstracting away multi-language parsing.

plannedNodes.name: GraphBuilder
plannedNodes.purpose: Topological relationship resolver and graph architect.
plannedNodes.description: GraphBuilder: Topological relationship resolver and graph architect. DESIGN INTENT: Serves as the "Skeleton" constructor for the system. It takes the flat list of scanned source files and transforms them into a directed hierarchical graph. It is responsible for resolving string-based dependencies into concrete node relationships and identifying architectural orphans. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Build the primary hierarchy based on entry points and gravity-based relationships. 2. Resolve 'Interstellar' dependencies (cross-module imports). 3. Event Bridge: Implicitly connects publishers and subscribers based on event names. 4. Orphan Identification: Groups all unreachable nodes into the virtual '_UNCONNECTED_' root.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Serves as the 'Skeleton' constructor for the system. It transforms scanned source files into a directed hierarchical graph, resolving dependencies and identifies orphans.

plannedNodes.name: PolarLayoutStrategy
plannedNodes.purpose: Mathematical projection and spatial positioning engine.
plannedNodes.description: PolarLayoutStrategy: Mathematical projection and spatial positioning engine. DESIGN INTENT: Implements the visual "Soul" of the Atlas project. It translates the abstract tree-like hierarchy of the graph into concrete 2D coordinates using a polar coordinate system. By distributing nodes in wedges around their parents, it ensures a non-overlapping, expansive layout that emphasizes hierarchy and 'Mass'. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Calculates node coordinates using a Wedge-based distribution algorithm. 2. Manages radial depth based on the topological distance from roots. 3. Supports manual coordinate overrides for persistent layouts. 4. Ensures hierarchical stability by sorting nodes by 'Mass' (descendant count).
plannedNodes.designIntent: Implements the visual 'Soul' of the Atlas project. It translates the abstract tree hierarchy into concrete 2D coordinates using a polar wedge distribution algorithm.

plannedNodes.name: Config
plannedNodes.purpose: Architectural ruleset and system configuration.
plannedNodes.description: IAtlasConfig: The architectural ruleset and system configuration. DESIGN INTENT: Serves as the "Foundational Mandate" for an Atlas project. It defines the scanning boundaries and, most importantly, the architectural constraints (rules) that the engine uses to detect "Iron Law" violations. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Defines project entry points and file inclusion exclusion patterns. 2. Houses the Architectural Ruleset used for automated linting. 3. Configures environmental properties like the service port.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Serves as the 'Foundational Mandate' for an Atlas project. It defines the scanning boundaries and architectural constraints used for automated linting.

plannedNodes.name: pipeline
plannedNodes.purpose: Task lifecycle and synchronization state manager.
plannedNodes.description: PipelineManager: Task lifecycle and synchronization state manager. DESIGN INTENT: Provides the "Muscles" of the project management layer. It automates the synchronization between the scanned 'Reality' and the 'Planned' blueprint by generating actionable Markdown tasks for architectural drift or missing implementations. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Generates 'Ghost Node' tasks for nodes planned in the blueprint but missing in code. 2. Generates 'Audit' tasks for implementation changes that violate previous verifications. 3. Manages the 5-stage task lifecycle (Backlog -> Todo -> In Progress -> Review -> Completed). 4. Ensures only 1 active task is in progress per the Iron Law.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provides the 'Muscles' of the project management layer. It automates the synchronization between scanned reality and the blueprint by generating Markdown tasks.

plannedNodes.name: blueprint
plannedNodes.purpose: Intentional architecture and blueprint manager.
plannedNodes.description: TopologyPlanner: Intentional architecture and evolution manager. DESIGN INTENT: Manages two states of the architecture: 1. Authoritative (blueprint.json): The verified, current structural mandate. 2. Plan (plan.json): The draft board for planning future work. Provides CLI-driven "Merge" to move plan into the authoritative blueprint.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provides the 'Visions' of the project. It manages the planned.json file, serving as the authoritative source for intentional topology and drift detection.

plannedNodes.name: Broadcaster
plannedNodes.purpose: Real-time synchronization hub.
plannedNodes.description: Broadcaster: Real-time synchronization hub. DESIGN INTENT: Provides the "Nervous System" of the Atlas service. It enables immediate feedback loops by pushing server-side events (SSE) to all connected visualizers. It ensures that when a scan completes or a command is issued, the UI reflects the new state without a manual refresh. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Manages a registry of active Server-Sent Events (SSE) client connections. 2. Provides a simplified broadcast interface for system-wide event propagation. 3. Handles initial keep-alive signals and automatic client cleanup on disconnect.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provides the 'Nervous System' of the Atlas service. It enables immediate feedback loops by pushing server-side events (SSE) to all connected visualizers.

plannedNodes.name: Viewer Entry
plannedNodes.purpose: Frontend Composition Root and bootstrapper
plannedNodes.description: Viewer Main: The primary entry point and Composition Root for the frontend visualizer. It orchestrates the lifecycle of the D3 engine, renderer, and UI components.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Act as the central hub for the visualizer, managing state transitions and wiring up components while providing a clean separation between the data layer and the UI layer.

plannedNodes.name: UI Toolbar
plannedNodes.purpose: Global toolkit for sync, legend, and mode switching
plannedNodes.description: Toolbar: The global toolkit for the visualizer. It provides buttons for syncing reality, toggling the legend, and switching between Blueprint and Planning modes.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provide a consolidated and easily accessible interface for all visualizer-level actions, following a consistent UI pattern.

plannedNodes.name: Theme Manager
plannedNodes.purpose: Central authority for visual styles and geometric definitions
plannedNodes.description: ThemeManager: The central authority for visual styles and geometric definitions. it defines colors, shapes, and animations for the visualizer.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Decouple visual styling from the rendering logic, allowing for easy theme changes and consistent look-and-feel across all components.

plannedNodes.name: Visual Protocol
plannedNodes.purpose: Shared types and interfaces for the visualizer engine
plannedNodes.description: VisualProtocol: Defines the shared types and interfaces used by the visualizer engine, ensuring type safety between the backend data and frontend components.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Standardize the data contract for visual nodes and links to facilitate seamless communication between the engine, renderer, and UI components.

plannedNodes.name: System Telemetry
plannedNodes.purpose: Centralized logging and metrics provider
plannedNodes.description: TelemetryProvider: Centralized logging and telemetry service. It collects system-wide metrics and logs for analysis.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provide a single point of entry for all logging and metric collection to ensure consistent data format and easy integration with external monitoring tools.

plannedNodes.name: Security Service
plannedNodes.purpose: Integrity and access validation service
plannedNodes.description: SecurityService: Responsible for system integrity and access validation. It ensures that only authorized operations are performed.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Protect the system's core data structures and ensure that architectural constraints are enforced at the operation level.

plannedNodes.name: Galaxy Engine
plannedNodes.purpose: Data flow and simulation orchestrator
plannedNodes.description: GalaxyEngine: Orchestrates the visualizer's data flow and the D3 force simulation. It manages node spawning and position updates.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Centralize the D3 simulation lifecycle and data management to ensure a high-performance and reactive graph experience.

plannedNodes.name: Physics Engine
plannedNodes.purpose: D3 force configurations for layout
plannedNodes.description: GalaxyPhysics: Contains the mathematical configurations for D3 forces (gravity, collision, links). It defines the spatial behavior of the graph.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Encapsulate physics parameters to allow for easy fine-tuning of the topological layout without affecting the core engine logic.

plannedNodes.name: Stage Renderer
plannedNodes.purpose: D3 canvas rendering engine
plannedNodes.description: StageRenderer: The D3-based canvas rendering engine. it draws nodes, links, and manages user camera interactions (pan/zoom).
plannedNodes.designIntent: Provide a robust and scalable canvas implementation that handles complex geometric rendering and provides smooth interactive feedback.

plannedNodes.name: Node Inspector
plannedNodes.purpose: Sidebar for node metadata editing
plannedNodes.description: Inspector: A UI component that renders detailed metadata for the currently selected node, allowing for real-time attribute editing.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Enable deep-tissue inspection and editing of architectural intent directly from the visualizer.

plannedNodes.name: UI Legend
plannedNodes.purpose: Legend for node types and connections
plannedNodes.description: Legend: Displays a visual guide for the user, mapping geometric shapes and colors to specific node types and connection rules.
plannedNodes.designIntent: Self-document the architectural visualization language to ensure that the graph is interpretable by any user.

plannedNodes.name: FinalTest
plannedNodes.purpose: Functional sentinel for Atlas Engine health and SSE stability.
